title: Griffitharia guanii
intro: Griffitharia guanii, also known as Andean Griffonia, is a plant species native to South America, characterized by its climbing habit and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce anxiety and stress due to its high content of 5-hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP), an amino acid that plays a role in serotonin production in the brain. The bioactive constituents of Griffitharia guanii include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Griffitharia guanii is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as supplements and teas, that aim to support mood balance and reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety.
taxonomy: Griffitharia guanii is classified as a species within the genus Griffitharia, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Griffitharia guanii is a type of orchid native to South America. The genus Griffitharia is a small group of terrestrial orchids, and Griffitharia guanii is one of its members.
